spec_match.rationale: Calls real `navigator.mediaDevices.setPreferredSinkId(...)` then plays a tone and reads back `audio.sinkId` and `AudioContext.destination.sinkId` to verify. That is the headline method the spec adds.

interactive.rationale: Working select (populated from `enumerateDevices`) + buttons drive both an `<audio>` element path and a WebAudio destination path side by side with a feature-detect probe.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index says `Set the default audio output for the whole top-level frame ... pick one, then play tones and inspect sinkId on each element`; the page does exactly that with two element types.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers two output types but never demonstrates the empty-string default sink, what happens with an invalid/no-longer-present deviceId, or device unplug between selection and play.

references.rationale: `see also` links chromestatus 5098834847072256 and the mediacapture-output spec.

design_system.rationale: 44 CSS variable references, no raw hex, no button override mismatch.

openQuestions.title: Show the empty-deviceId default-sink behaviour
openQuestions.detail: `setPreferredSinkId('')` resets to the system default. Add an explicit `Reset to default` button so the round-trip from chosen→default is visible, and confirm `sinkId` becomes the empty string after.

openQuestions.title: Handle device unplug between selection and play
openQuestions.detail: If a Bluetooth output disconnects after selection but before playback, the call should fail / fall back. Either listen for `devicechange` and surface a banner, or attempt `play()` on a stale ID and surface the resulting error so the failure mode is documented.

openQuestions.title: Surface the invalid-deviceId rejection path
openQuestions.detail: Add a `try invalid id` button that calls `setPreferredSinkId('not-a-real-device-id')` and prints the rejection — the spec's error contract should be visible.

summary: Strong canonical demo of the new method against both `<audio>` and WebAudio destinations with the right references. Round out with the empty-string default-reset path, device-change handling, and the invalid-id rejection — the three failure modes the spec explicitly addresses.
